rulings.Case Title: CHARLES LI VS. MEI LING FANG ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Service Of Summons And Set Aside Default
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for December 2, 2022 line 1. DEFENDANT SHUZHEN TU Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Service Of Summons And Set Aside Default is continued to December 28, 2022. No later than December 21, 2022 Defendant to file a supplemental reply not to exceed 3 pages addressing the opposition on the merits, specifically Warner Bros v. Golden W. Music Sales (1974) 36 Cal.App.3d 1012. =(501/REQ) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (551) 285-1373; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: MICHELLE BAUMERT VS. KATHERINE O'BRIEN ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for December 2, 2022 line 8. DEFENDANT KATHERINE O'BRIEN DEMURRER to COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED. Demurrer to the sole cause of action for unlawful detainer currently alleged in the complaint is sustained without leave to amend. Notice fails to comply with ALL requirements of SFRO 37.9(a)(8)(v) and Regulation 12.14(b)(8). To the extent Plaintiff wishes to amend the complaint to state a cause of action for ejectment, Plaintiff shall have a 10-day leave to amend to do so and obtain a 30-day summons applicable to general (non-summary) civil cases. =(501/REQ) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (551) 285-1373; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.
